statement: Oklahoma requires private process servers to be licensed under 12 O.S. § 158.1. Any person who knowingly and willfully serves process without a license is guilty of a misdemeanor.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: Any person who knowingly and willfully serves process in Oklahoma without a process server license issued by the State of Oklahoma or who holds himself or herself out to be a process server licensed by the State of Oklahoma when the person is not licensed as such shall, upon conviction, be guilty of a misdemeanor.

statement: To obtain an Oklahoma process server license, applicant must be 18+, of good moral character, ethically and mentally fit, Oklahoma resident for 6+ months, and county resident for 30+ days.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: Any person who is: 1. Eighteen (18) years of age or older; 2. Of good moral character; 3. Found ethically and mentally fit; 4. A resident of the State of Oklahoma for a period of not less than six (6) months; and 5. A resident of the county or judicial administrative district in which the application is submitted for a period of not less than thirty (30) days, may obtain a license by filing an application with the court clerk on a verified form.

statement: Persons convicted of a violent crime (as defined in 57 O.S. § 571) or any crime requiring sex offender registration are prohibited from obtaining or renewing a process server license in Oklahoma.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: Any person who has been convicted of a violent crime, as defined in Section 571 of Title 57 of the Oklahoma Statutes, or a crime that requires the person to register pursuant to the Sex Offenders Registration Act in this state or another state shall be prohibited from applying for or obtaining a license to serve process in this state.

statement: The statewide process server license fee is $150.00 plus regular docketing, posting, mailing, and filing fees. Initial license is valid for 1 year, then renewable for 3-year periods at $15 per renewal.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: The applicant filing for a license to serve process anywhere in this state shall pay a license fee of One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150.00), and the regular docketing, posting, mailing, and filing fees prescribed by law... At the end of one (1) calendar year from the date of issuance of the initial license, the license shall be renewed for a period of three (3) years... A fee of Fifteen Dollars ($15.00) per renewal shall be charged for each statewide license renewal.

statement: A $5,000 surety bond running to the State of Oklahoma is required for faithful performance of duties before a process server license is granted.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: upon executing bond running to the State of Oklahoma in the amount of Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000.00) for faithful performance of his or her duties and filing the bond with the court clerk, the applicant shall be authorized and licensed to serve court documents in any county in this state.

statement: Tulsa County charges $364.14 for a new statewide process server license (including all fees), $229.14 for renewals, and requires a $35 publication fee within 10 days of application.
source_title: Tulsa County Court Clerk - Family Department (Private Process Server Licensing)
quote: New license filing fee is $364.14; Renewals are $229.14; You will also be required to publish a notice within 10 days of application. There is a $35.00 fee.

statement: Tulsa County requires new process server applicants to: secure a $5,000 bond, wait a 30-day posting period, provide two 2x2 photographs. First-time applicants receive a 1-year license; successive renewals are for 3 years.
source_title: Tulsa County Court Clerk - Family Department (Private Process Server Licensing)
quote: In order to receive a license as a new process server an individual must: Secure a $5,000 bond for the new license; Wait a 30 day posting period; A first time applicant will be issued a 1 year license; Every successive renewal thereafter will be issued for 3 years; Applicants must bring two (2) copies of a 2'x2' photograph.

statement: Oklahoma County (and other counties) follow the same statutory framework under 12 O.S. § 158.1 with statewide license. McCurtain County charges $364.14 new / $229.14 renewal for statewide license.
source_title: McCurtain County - Private Process Server Licensing
quote: New Process Server - Statewide $364.14 - Good for one (1) year / Process is the same as a new license/Statewide; Renewal Process Server - Statewide $229.14 - Good for three (3) years / Process is the same as a new license/Statewide

statement: Oklahoma service of process is governed by 12 O.S. § 2004. Personal delivery is the primary method; substituted service may be made by leaving copies at the dwelling with a person 15+ years old residing therein.
source_title: Oklahoma Statutes §12-2004 (2025) - Process
quote: upon an individual other than an infant who is less than fifteen (15) years of age or an incompetent person, by delivering a copy of the summons and of the petition personally or by leaving copies thereof at the person's dwelling house or usual place of abode with some person then residing therein who is fifteen (15) years of age or older

statement: Service of summons must be made within 180 days after filing of the petition. If not served within 180 days without good cause, the action is deemed dismissed without prejudice.
source_title: Oklahoma Statutes §12-2004 (2025) - Process
quote: If service of process is not made upon a defendant within one hundred eighty (180) days after the filing of the petition and the plaintiff has not shown good cause why such service was not made within that period, the action shall be deemed dismissed as to that defendant without prejudice.

statement: Proof of service must be shown by affidavit and must set forth the county of issuance, name of person served, date, place, and method of service.
source_title: Oklahoma Statutes §12-2004 (2025) - Process
quote: If service is made by a person other than a sheriff or deputy sheriff, the licensed process server shall make affidavit thereof. The return shall set forth the county of issuance, the name of the person served and the date, place and method of service.

statement: No prisoner in any jail, Department of Corrections facility, private prison, or parolee or probationer under supervision of the Department of Corrections shall be appointed by any court to serve process.
source_title: Oklahoma Statutes §12-2004 (2025) - Process
quote: No prisoner in any jail, Department of Corrections facility, private prison, or parolee or probationer under supervision of the Department of Corrections shall be appointed by any court to serve process on any defendant, party or witness.

statement: In Oklahoma, there are no legal restrictions on the days or hours when process servers can serve legal documents; they are authorized to serve documents 24/7. Industry best practice recommends reasonable hours (approximately 6 AM to 10 PM).
source_title: Understanding Process Service Regulations and Restrictions in Oklahoma
quote: In Oklahoma, unlike some states, there are no legal restrictions on the days or hours when process servers can serve legal documents. They are authorized to serve documents 24/7. However, it's important to note that process servers typically attempt service during reasonable hours, around 6 AM to 10 PM, to maximize their chances of finding the recipient and to avoid potential safety concerns.

statement: Process servers in Oklahoma cannot: trespass on private property without permission, impersonate law enforcement, harass or intimidate recipients, serve warrants or arrest documents, or break into homes or businesses.
source_title: Understanding Process Service Regulations and Restrictions in Oklahoma
quote: Geographic restrictions: County-licensed process servers can only serve process within their authorized county. Statewide licenses allow service throughout Oklahoma. Service limitations: Process servers cannot serve warrants, arrest warrants, or other documents that authorize taking property or arresting someone. Conduct restrictions: Process servers cannot harass, intimidate, or impersonate law enforcement while serving process.

statement: A process server in Oklahoma cannot force their way onto private property without the owner's consent. If consent is denied, the server must leave and find another method of service.
source_title: Learn How Far Can a Process Server Go in Piedmont, Okc?
quote: No, a process server in Oklahoma City cannot force its way onto private property without the owner's consent. If the legal agent in OKC does not have consent and they try to enter private property, they could be charged with trespassing.

statement: NAPPS (National Association of Professional Process Servers) Code of Ethics requires members to: perform all work lawfully and professionally, keep current all licenses/bonds/permits, promptly return proofs of service, promptly pay for services rendered by other members, and maintain confidentiality of clients.
source_title: NAPPS Membership Code Of Ethics
quote: All work shall be performed in a lawful, professional and ethical manner... Each member agrees to comply with and keep current during the tenure of his membership all necessary business licenses, bonds, permits and any other requirements mandated by all jurisdictions in which the member conducts business... All proofs of service shall be returned promptly upon completing the work order... Each member agrees to promptly pay for services rendered by another member unless other specific arrangements have been made.

statement: NAPPS Best Practices state that service of process should be performed by a disinterested third party; in-person delivery to the named party is the preferred method; a written permanent record of service should be maintained and made available upon request.
source_title: NAPPS Process Server Best Practices And Standards
quote: Service of process or other papers for the purpose of acquiring jurisdiction over a person or property should be performed by a disinterested third party. The preferred and most effective method of service of process upon a party is in-person delivery of process to the named party... A written permanent record of the service should be maintained, and made available upon request.

statement: NAPPS Best Practices require that a proof or affidavit of service must accurately state the date, time, place, and manner of service. It is not proper for a proof to be signed before completion or signed in blank.
source_title: NAPPS Process Server Best Practices And Standards
quote: A proof or affidavit of service must accurately state the date, time, place, and manner of service, and any additional information that would reflect how delivery of process or other legal document was made to a person or entity served... It is not proper for a proof of service to be signed before completion, or signed in blank to be completed later. It is not permissible to sign the process server's name to a proof of service on his or her behalf.

statement: NAPPS membership requirements: no felony convictions (unless expunged/pardoned), affiliated with process serving for minimum 1 year, must abide by NAPPS Bylaws and Code of Ethics, provide valid driver's license, $175 annual dues plus $25 application fee.
source_title: How To Become A Process Serving Member of NAPPS
quote: No felony convictions that have not been expunged or pardoned. Affiliated with the process serving profession for a minimum of one year. Must abide by the NAPPS Bylaws and Code of Ethics. Provide a copy of a valid driver's license. Pay $175.00 annual dues and $25.00 application fee.

statement: Joseph Iannazzi is a NAPPS member with Member ID 14801, joined in 2025. He operates Just Legal Solutions in Glenpool, Oklahoma and serves Tulsa, Wagoner, Rogers, and Osage counties primarily.
source_title: National Association of Professional Process Servers - Member Profile
quote: Name: Iannazzi, Joseph; Member Since: 2025; Mailing: Just Legal Solutions 564 E 138th Pl Glenpool, Oklahoma 74033; Services: PS CF CRS PC ST (Fee in advance) E-Filing, Courier; Counties Served: Tulsa, Wagoner, Rogers, Osage

statement: Joseph Iannazzi is a licensed Oklahoma process server (12 O.S. § 158.1), commissioned Notary Public, certified Remote Online Notary, NAPPS Member ID 14801, Snapdocs-verified, and founder of Just Legal Solutions.
source_title: Joseph Iannazzi | Founder & CEO, Just Legal Solutions
quote: Licensed and bonded Oklahoma process server, commissioned Notary Public and Remote Online Notary, NAPPS Member ID 14801, Snapdocs-verified, Northeastern State University alumnus, and founder of Oklahoma's most transparent process serving and mobile notary firm.

statement: NAPPS Position on Photography/Video: Due to serious privacy and safety concerns, process servers should not be required to photograph or videotape the subjects they are serving. Recording individuals while being served can violate their privacy rights.
source_title: NAPPS Process Server Best Practices And Standards - Position on Photography or Video as Proof of Service
quote: In response to this trend, the National Association of Professional Process Servers has sought the advice of counsel and now takes the position that, in light of serious privacy and safety concerns, process servers should not be required to photograph or videotape the subjects they are being served. A legal assessment of this practice demonstrates that recording individuals while they are being served can violate their privacy rights.

statement: GPS tracking is widely recommended as a best practice in the process serving industry for documenting the exact location of service attempts, providing irrefutable evidence of compliance.
source_title: Best Practices for Documenting Attempts to Serve Legal Papers
quote: GPS Tracking: Recording the exact location of service attempts provides irrefutable evidence of compliance. Digital Cameras: Photos of the property or attempted service location add visual context to documentation.

statement: Professional liability insurance (E&O) is not statutorily required for Oklahoma process servers but is strongly recommended. Industry sources suggest $100,000-$500,000 in liability coverage and note that bonds only protect the state, not the server.
source_title: Why Oklahoma Process Servers Should Carry Professional Liability Insurance
quote: Most states require process servers, private investigators, and notary public officials to carry some type of a bond. Many Oklahoma inquiry agent, mobile notaries, and Oklahoma process servers might unknowingly assume that a bond covers them in all ways. This is simply not true, as bonds only serve to help protect their respective states from liability... A notary public in Oklahoma is not required to do so, but most of the major signing companies will not contact them and utilize their services unless they carry anywhere between $25,000-$100,000 of errors and omissions (E&O) insurance.

statement: Industry best practices recommend documenting every service attempt with: date and time, exact address/location, description of environment, interaction details (names, descriptions, statements), weather conditions, and immediate recording after the attempt.
source_title: Best Practices for Documenting Attempts to Serve Legal Papers
quote: Date and Time of Each Attempt... Location of the Attempt: The exact address where the attempt occurred should be recorded... Description of the Environment... Interaction Details: Document any interactions with individuals at the location... Weather and Conditions... Record Immediately: Document service attempts as soon as they occur to ensure accuracy.

statement: NAPPS was founded in 1982, is headquartered in Portland, Oregon, and represents over 2,000 members across all 50 U.S. states, the District of Columbia, seven Canadian provinces, and nearly two dozen foreign countries.
source_title: NAPPS: National Association of Professional Process Servers
quote: Founded in 1982 in Las Vegas NV, NAPPS today has well over 2,000 members representing all 50 states, the District of Columbia, seven Canadian provinces and nearly two dozen foreign countries. It is headquartered in Portland, Oregon.

statement: Process servers should make attempts on each job within 48 hours of receipt and within every 48 hours after, per industry guidelines. Rush orders require attempts within 24 hours.
source_title: ABC Legal - Attempt Reporting Requirements
quote: A process server shall make attempts on each job within 48 hours of receipt and within every 48 hours after. For Rush orders attempts must be made within 24 hours of claiming the order and every 24 hours after.

statement: If a process server license is revoked in Oklahoma, the licensee cannot reapply for 5 years. If revoked once, renewal fee is $1,000. A second revocation results in permanent inability to renew.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: If the license is revoked, the licensee shall not be permitted to reapply for a license for a period of five (5) years from the date of revocation. Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, any licensee whose license has been revoked one time shall pay the sum of One Thousand Dollars ($1,000.00) as a renewal fee. If a second revocation occurs, the chief or presiding judge shall not allow an applicant to renew the license.

statement: License revocation proceedings may be initiated by the district attorney or Attorney General, alleging violation of law. The licensee receives at least 30 days notice by certified mail, and the hearing is before the chief or presiding judge without a jury.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: The district attorney of the county wherein a license authorized under this act has been issued or the Attorney General may file a petition in the district court to revoke the license... After at least thirty (30) days of notice by certified mail to the licensee, the chief or presiding judge, sitting without jury, shall hear the petition and enter an order thereon.

statement: Upon application filing, the court clerk gives 30 days notice by posting on the county website or courthouse. Applicant must publish notice once in a legal newspaper at least 20 days before hearing. Notice is also sent to district attorney, sheriff, OSBI, and AOC.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: the court clerk shall give thirty (30) days of notice of hearing by causing the notice to be continually posted for thirty (30) days on the website of the county, or be posted in the courthouse. The applicant shall cause notice of the hearing to be made no less than twenty (20) days prior to the hearing one time by publication in a legal newspaper... The court clerk shall deliver by electronic means or deliver a copy of the notice at least twenty (20) days prior to the hearing to the district attorney, the sheriff... the Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigation and the Administrative Office of the Courts.

statement: The Administrative Office of the Courts maintains a statewide registry of licensed private process servers, accessible to the public. The court clerk must also make available a list of licensed private process servers in their office.
source_title: 2025 Oklahoma Statutes :: Title 12. Civil Procedure :: §12-158.1. Private process servers
quote: The court clerk shall make available at all times in the office of the court clerk the list of licensed private process servers. Any person in need of the services of a process server may designate one from the names on the list... No later than January 1, 2013, the Administrative Office of the Courts shall establish and maintain a statewide registry which shall contain a list of licensed private process servers.

statement: Oklahoma process servers can serve various legal documents including: subpoenas, small claims affidavits, evictions, petitions for replevin, indirect contempt citations, victim protection orders, hearings on assets, property foreclosures, temporary restraining orders, civil rights lawsuits, car accident lawsuits, child custody matters, and divorce papers.
source_title: Process Server in Tulsa, Oklahoma FAQs
quote: The types of papers that a process server in Norman, OK and a private detective near me at a private investigation agency near me often serves the following types of court and other legal documents: Subpoenas, Small Claims Affidavits and Orders, Evictions, 5-Day Notices to Pay or Quit, Petitions for Replevin, Indirect Contempt Citations, Victim Protection Orders (VPOs), Hearings on Assets (HoAs), Property Foreclosures, Temporary Restraining Orders (TROs), Civil Rights Lawsuits, Car Accident/Car Injury Lawsuits, Child Custody Matters, Divorce Packets, Demand Letters

statement: Process servers may not serve inmates in Department of Corrections facilities directly. Service must be made to the warden or superintendent, who then delivers the documents to the inmate. The warden must reject service if the inmate is not actually present.
source_title: Oklahoma Statutes §12-2004 (2025) - Process
quote: upon an inmate incarcerated in an institution under the jurisdiction and control of the Department of Corrections, by delivering a copy of the summons and of the petition to the warden or superintendent or the designee of the warden or superintendent... It shall be the duty of the receiving warden or superintendent or a designee to promptly deliver the summons and petition to the inmate named therein. The warden or superintendent or his or her designee shall reject service of process for any inmate who is not actually present in the institution.

statement: No specific Oklahoma state-level process server association (independent from NAPPS) was identified during research. The primary professional organization for Oklahoma process servers is NAPPS, which has multiple members in the Tulsa and Oklahoma City areas.
source_title: Oklahoma State-Level Process Server Associations
quote: No independent Oklahoma state-level process server association website or organization was found. NAPPS serves as the primary national organization with Oklahoma members. Oklahoma Judicial Process Servers (oklahomajudicialprocessservers.com) appears to be a private business, not a trade association.

statement: Skip tracing is a service offered by professional process servers and NAPPS members. It must be conducted within legal bounds under applicable federal and state laws including the Fair Credit Reporting Act and Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act.
source_title: NAPPS Member Profile - Joseph Iannazzi (Services Listed)
quote: Services: PS CF CRS PC ST (Fee in advance) E-Filing, Courier [ST = Skip Tracing per NAPPS key]
